events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need analyze scenario: Cable tray industrial site, missing bonding across expansion joints. Work rough-complete. Reference NEC Art 392. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Missing bonding across expansion joints violates NEC 392.60? Let's recall NEC Article 392 Cable Trays. Section 392.60 Grounding and Bonding. Cable tray systems shall be electrically continuous and grounded per Article 250.96? Actually 392.60(A) says metal cable trays shall be grounded as required in Article 250 and shall comply with 392.60(A)(1) through (A)(4). Expansion joints? There is 392.60(B) Bonding. Each run of cable tray shall be electrically continuous; mechanical discontinuities, such as expansion joints, shall be bonded to maintain electrical continuity. Bonding jumpers maybe required across expansion joints. Missing bonding means discontinuity, not electrically continuous, so fail. Risk high due to shock/arc flash/fire in industrial site. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 85? rough-complete. Value maybe number of missing bonds? Not asked numeric reading; use 0. Findings: missing bonding jumpers across expansion joints; loss of electrical continuity; shock/arc flash hazard. Actions: install listed bonding jumpers across each expansion joint per NEC 392.60(B) and project specs; verify continuity; inspect/re-test. Rationale. References: NEC Art. 392.60(B), 250.96 maybe. Need output exactly one valid JSON object. Ensure no prose outside.

Missing bonding jumpers leaves the tray electrically discontinuous and non-compliant, creating a serious safety hazard in an industrial environment.",

  ]
